mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase borrowing costs for corporations and consumers, directly reducing expected future cash flows and compressing equity valuations, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant negative correlation with rate increases.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0 percent, consumer spending power deteriorates rapidly, triggering corporate earnings downgrades that force negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below random chance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts signal a broad contraction in consumer demand, which reduces corporate revenue growth and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following month.
failure_condition: The joint decline in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative 20-day returns with a p-value below 0.05, or the interaction term loses explanatory power when added to a baseline regression model.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during post-recession recovery phases indicates expanding manufacturing capacity and rising corporate profits, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns during recovery periods, or the mechanism direction flips to negative when tested against historical recovery episodes.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 205, inflation expectations become unanchored, prompting markets to price in aggressive monetary tightening that produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in over 60 percent of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 205, or the threshold effect disappears when controlling for concurrent Fed policy announcements.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, which boosts consumer confidence and corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS and UNRATE signal yields a negative or zero Rank IC over rolling 3-year windows, or fails to outperform a single-proxy baseline in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product reflects stronger aggregate economic activity, which directly increases corporate profit margins and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth shows no statistically significant positive correlation with 60-day forward returns, or the predictive relationship reverses during periods of financial stress.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production surpasses 110, manufacturing output reaches capacity constraints that support pricing power and margin expansion, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day returns are negative in more than 50 percent of instances where INDPRO exceeds 110, or the threshold signal loses predictive power when adjusted for seasonal factors.
